title: The Lenape make Lenapehoking their homeland
note: Authentic 1915 photograph of Lenape (Delaware) people; no period image can exist for a c.1000 event, and this is a genuine old photo of the actual people, not a modern skyline.

title: Verrazzano sails into New York Harbor
note: Current image is the modern Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge. Replaced with a 1768 engraved portrait of Verrazzano.

title: Estêvão Gomes charts the coast for Spain
note: Genuine 1529 Diogo Ribeiro world map, exactly the period cartography described in the blurb.

title: Henry Hudson explores the river that bears his name
note: Traditional engraved portrait of Hudson; a historical depiction, not a modern photo.

title: Adriaen Block winters on Manhattan after the Tyger burns
note: Current image is a modern Wikipedia-user reconstruction map (Wpdms), not a genuine period image; Block's actual 1614 chart is not readily available on Commons.

title: New Netherland Company chartered; Fort Nassau built
note: Detail of a c.1630 Rensselaerswyck map showing Fort Orange — genuine period cartography.

title: Dutch West India Company is chartered
note: Current image is a modern SVG rendering of the company flag; no clean genuine period engraving of the company/West India House readily available.

title: First settlers arrive aboard the Nieu Nederlandt
commonsFile: File:The First View of New York 1626-8.jpg
note: Current image is a modern SVG of the Prince's flag. Replaced with the Hartgers View (c.1626), the earliest known view of New Amsterdam — perfect for the founding settlers.

title: Fort Amsterdam rises at the tip of Manhattan
note: Period depiction of Fort Amsterdam, 1660.

title: The Schaghen letter records the 'purchase' of Manhattan
note: The Castello Plan (1660) is a genuine period map of New Amsterdam; period-appropriate even if not the letter itself.

title: First enslaved Africans brought to New Amsterdam
note: Howard Pyle historical illustration of an early New Amsterdam slave sale; an old artistic depiction, not a modern photo. No genuine 1620s image exists.

title: Charter of Freedoms and Exemptions creates patroonships
note: Image of the 1630 Charter document itself — genuine period source.

title: Wouter van Twiller becomes director-general
note: Period portrait of Van Twiller.

title: Jonas Bronck settles the land that became the Bronx
note: Historical illustration of Bronck signing his treaty with the Lenape; an old depiction, not a modern photo.

title: First ferry links Manhattan and Brooklyn
commonsFile: File:Manatvs gelegen op de Noot Riuier.jpg
note: Current image is a modern 5-boroughs locator SVG. Replaced with the Manatus Map (1639), a genuine period map showing Manhattan, Long Island and the ferry crossing.

title: Kieft's War erupts with the Pavonia massacre
note: Historical illustration of the Pavonia massacre; period-appropriate depiction of the event.

title: Deborah Moody founds Gravesend
note: Current image is a modern photo of Gravesend Bay; no genuine 17th-century image of Gravesend or Moody is readily available.

title: Treaty ends Kieft's War
note: Same Pavonia massacre illustration; an era-appropriate historical depiction of Kieft's War.

title: Peter Stuyvesant arrives as director-general
note: Genuine c.1660 portrait of Stuyvesant.

title: New Amsterdam gains a municipal government
note: Genuine period view of New Amsterdam.

title: A defensive wall gives Wall Street its name
commonsFile: File:Stad Amsterdam in Nieuw Nederland (City Amsterdam in New Netherland) Castello Plan 1660.jpg
note: Current image is a modern photo of today's Wall Street. Replaced with the Castello Plan (1660), which depicts the Dutch defensive wall along the town's northern edge.

title: First Jewish refugees reach New Amsterdam
note: Genuine period view of New Amsterdam.

title: The Peach War strikes New Amsterdam
note: Novi Belgii map (Amsterdam, 1685) — genuine period cartography of New Netherland.

title: The Flushing Remonstrance defends religious liberty
note: Image of the 1657 Flushing Remonstrance document itself — genuine period source.

title: Stuyvesant charters Nieuw Haarlem
note: Current image is a modern photo of present-day Harlem; no genuine 17th-century image of Nieuw Haarlem is readily available.

title: The Castello Plan maps a town of 1,500
note: The Castello Plan (1660) itself — the exact subject and genuine period.

title: The English seize New Amsterdam, renaming it New York
note: Current image is a modern Empire State Building skyline photo. Replaced with the Duke's Plan (1664), the English map of the city made in the very year of the conquest.

title: The English seize New Amsterdam and rename it New York
note: Current image is a modern Empire State Building skyline photo. Replaced with the Duke's Plan (1664), the English map of the city from the year of conquest.

title: The Duke's Laws codify English rule
commonsFile: File:James II when Duke of York by Peter Lely.jpg
note: Current image is a modern photo of a historical marker. Replaced with a c.1665 Peter Lely portrait of James, Duke of York, the proprietor whose laws these were.

title: The Dutch retake the city and rename it New Orange
note: Detail of Allard's 17th-century Totius Neobelgii map — genuine period cartography.

title: The Treaty of Westminster returns New York to England
note: Detail of Allard's 17th-century map — genuine period cartography.

title: The Bolting Act hands the city a flour monopoly
note: Manatus Map (1639) — genuine period map of the colony.

title: New York's first assembly passes a Charter of Liberties
note: Current image is a modern New York state flag SVG. Replaced with a portrait of Governor Thomas Dongan, who convened the 1683 assembly that passed the charter.

title: The Dongan Charter incorporates New York City
note: Current image is a modern New York City flag SVG. Replaced with a portrait of Governor Thomas Dongan, who issued the 1686 charter.

title: Leisler's Rebellion seizes control of the colony
note: Image of Leisler's 1689 Declaration document — genuine period source.

title: Jacob Leisler is executed for treason
note: Leisler's Declaration document; period-appropriate to the Leisler episode.

title: Trinity Church receives its royal charter
note: Current image is the wrong document (an 1827 King's College charter). No genuine period image of the 1697 first Trinity Church is readily available on Commons.

title: Captain Kidd is hanged for piracy
note: Current image is a Ferris painting of Blackbeard (wrong subject, c.1920). The only Kidd-specific Commons image is a 1933 romantic illustration, not genuine period; better to show none.

title: The 1712 slave revolt
note: Old print of the New York slave market c.1730 — an era-appropriate historical depiction.

title: The New-York Gazette, the city's first newspaper
note: Genuine 1729 front page of the New-York Gazette itself.

title: The Montgomerie Charter reorganizes city government
commonsFile: File:A view of Fort George with the city of New York, from the SW.jpg
note: Current image is a modern photo of Proprietary House in Perth Amboy, NJ (wrong place). Replaced with the Burgis/Carwitham view of Fort George and the city of New York (c.1731-36), genuine period imagery of the city at the charter's date.

title: The Mill Street Synagogue, first in North America
note: Current image is a modern photo of the Touro Synagogue in Newport (wrong building and place). No genuine period image of the Mill Street Synagogue is readily available.

title: Bowling Green, the city's first public park
commonsFile: File:A view of Fort George with the city of New York, from the SW.jpg
note: Current image is a modern photo. Replaced with the Burgis/Carwitham view of Fort George (c.1731-36); Bowling Green sits directly at Fort George, so this genuine period view shows the very spot near its founding.

title: The Zenger trial and freedom of the press
note: Historical illustration of the 1735 Zenger trial; an old depiction, not a modern photo.

title: The 1741 conspiracy trials
note: Historical illustration of the 1741 burnings; an era-appropriate depiction.

title: The New York Society Library is founded
note: Current image is a modern photo of the library's 1930s building. No genuine 1754-era image is readily available.

title: King's College, later Columbia, is founded
commonsFile: File:View of Columbia College in the city of New York (NYPL Hades-118289-54235).tif
note: Current image is a modern Columbia crown logo SVG. Replaced with a genuine 1790 engraved view of the actual King's/Columbia College building.

title: The Sons of Liberty organize in New York
note: Current image is a modern SVG flag. Replaced with a genuine 1766 British print on the Stamp Act repeal, the cause the Sons of Liberty led.

title: The Stamp Act Congress meets in New York
commonsFile: File:The repeal, or the funeral procession of Miss Americ-Stamp LCCN99471597.jpg
note: Current image is a modern 1776-campaign SVG map (wrong event). Replaced with a genuine 1766 satirical print on the Stamp Act.

title: The first Liberty Pole rises in the Common
note: Historical illustration of the Liberty Pole / Golden Hill confrontation; an old depiction of the events described.

title: The Battle of Golden Hill
note: Historical illustration of the 1770 Battle of Golden Hill.

title: The New York tea party
note: Current image is the modern Conservative Party of New York logo (entirely wrong). No genuine period image of the 1774 New York tea party is readily available.

title: The Declaration is read to Washington's troops
commonsFile: File:Dunlap broadside copy of the United States Declaration of Independence, LOC.jpg
note: Current image is Leutze's 1851 'Washington Crossing the Delaware' (wrong event, not period). Replaced with the Dunlap Broadside, the genuine July 1776 printing of the Declaration read to the troops.

title: The statue of King George III is toppled
commonsFile: File:Balthasar Friedrich Leizelt - Destruction of the Royal Statue in New York - Google Art Project.jpg
note: Current image is a modern photo of Bowling Green. Replaced with Leizelt's genuine 1770s engraving depicting the toppling of the George III statue in New York.

title: The Battle of Long Island
commonsFile: File:A plan of New York Island... (2674294469).jpg
note: Current image is a modern photo of Montauk. Replaced with a genuine 1776 map describing the engagement on the woody heights of Long Island.

title: The British land at Kip's Bay
note: Genuine 1776 print of the British landing at Kip's Bay.

title: The Battle of Harlem Heights
note: Historical depiction of the 1776 Battle of Harlem Heights (NYPL); an old illustration of the event.

title: The Great Fire of 1776
note: Genuine period engraving of the 1776 fire.

title: Nathan Hale is hanged as a spy
commonsFile: File:Last Words of Nathan Hale by Alexander Hay Ritchie.jpeg
note: Current image is a modern photo of the City Hall statue. Replaced with Ritchie's 1858 engraving of Hale's execution, a historical depiction of the event.

title: The Battle of White Plains
note: Genuine period engraving of the engagement at White Plains.

title: The fall of Fort Washington
note: Genuine 1776 aquatint (Thomas Davies) of the attack on Fort Washington.

title: The prison ships of Wallabout Bay
note: Period engraving of the HMS Jersey prison ship, 1782.

title: Evacuation Day ends British occupation
note: Historical print of Washington's 1783 triumphal entry; an old depiction of the event.

title: Washington's farewell at Fraunces Tavern
commonsFile: File:Washington taking leave of his officers (NYPL b12610213-421436).jpg
note: Current image is a modern photo of the reconstructed Fraunces Tavern. Replaced with a 19th-century engraving of Washington taking leave of his officers, a historical depiction of the 1783 scene.
